tool.name: nf-core/stableexpression
tool.description: This pipeline is dedicated to identifying the most stable genes within a single or multiple expression dataset(s). This is particularly useful for identifying the most suitable RT-qPCR reference genes for a specific species.
tool.command:
## ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ## Building new samplesheet with renamed files ## ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#if $input_output_options.datasets.provide_datasets == "true":
#set $count_filenames = '' #for $count_dataset in $input_output_options.datasets.count_datasets #set $count_filenames += ' ' + str($count_dataset.name) #end for
#set $design_filenames = '' #for $experimental_design in $input_output_options.datasets.experimental_designs #set $design_filenames += ' ' + str($experimental_design.name) #end for
python '${__tool_directory__}/rebuild_samplesheet.py' --in $input_output_options.datasets.samplesheet --out renamed_samplesheet.csv --count-files $input_output_options.datasets.count_datasets --count-filenames $count_filenames --design-files $input_output_options.datasets.experimental_designs --design-filenames $design_filenames
#else: echo "No user dataset provided"
#end if
## ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ## Running pipeline ## ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
&& nextflow run OlivierCoen/stableexpression -r dev -latest -profile micromamba -ansi-log false --outdir ./results --species "$input_output_options.species"
#if $input_output_options.datasets.provide_datasets == "true": --datasets renamed_samplesheet.csv #end if #if $input_output_options.keywords --keywords "$input_output_options.keywords" #end if #if $input_output_options.target_genes --target_genes "$input_output_options.target_genes" #end if #if $input_output_options.target_gene_file --target_gene_file "$input_output_options.target_gene_file" #end if #if $input_output_options.platform --platform "$input_output_options.platform" #end if #if $input_output_options.accessions_only --accessions_only $input_output_options.accessions_only #end if #if $input_output_options.download_only --download_only $input_output_options.download_only #end if #if $public_data_options.skip_fetch_eatlas_accessions --skip_fetch_eatlas_accessions $public_data_options.skip_fetch_eatlas_accessions #end if #if $public_data_options.fetch_geo_accessions --fetch_geo_accessions $public_data_options.fetch_geo_accessions #end if #if $public_data_options.accessions --accessions "$public_data_options.accessions" #end if #if $public_data_options.accessions_file --accessions_file "$public_data_options.accessions_file" #end if #if $public_data_options.excluded_accessions --excluded_accessions "$public_data_options.excluded_accessions" #end if #if $public_data_options.excluded_accessions_file --excluded_accessions_file "$public_data_options.excluded_accessions_file" #end if #if $idmapping_options.skip_id_mapping --skip_id_mapping $idmapping_options.skip_id_mapping #end if #if $idmapping_options.skip_cleaning_gene_ids --skip_cleaning_gene_ids $idmapping_options.skip_cleaning_gene_ids #end if #if $idmapping_options.gprofiler_target_db --gprofiler_target_db "$idmapping_options.gprofiler_target_db" #end if #if $idmapping_options.gene_id_mapping --gene_id_mapping "$idmapping_options.gene_id_mapping" #end if #if $idmapping_options.gene_metadata --gene_metadata "$idmapping_options.gene_metadata" #end if #if $idmapping_options.min_occurrence_quantile --min_occurrence_quantile $idmapping_options.min_occurrence_quantile #end if #if $idmapping_options.min_occurrence_freq --min_occurrence_freq $idmapping_options.min_occurrence_freq #end if #if $sample_filtering_options.max_zero_ratio --max_zero_ratio $sample_filtering_options.max_zero_ratio #end if #if $sample_filtering_options.max_null_ratio --max_null_ratio $sample_filtering_options.max_null_ratio #end if #if $sample_filtering_options.max_null_ratio_valid_sample --max_null_ratio_valid_sample $sample_filtering_options.max_null_ratio_valid_sample #end if --normalisation_method "$statistical_options.normalisation_method" #if $statistical_options.gff --gff "$statistical_options.gff" #end if #if $statistical_options.gene_length --gene_length "$statistical_options.gene_length" #end if #if $statistical_options.quantile_norm_target_distrib --quantile_norm_target_distrib "$statistical_options.quantile_norm_target_distrib" #end if #if $statistical_options.missing_value_imputer --missing_value_imputer "$statistical_options.missing_value_imputer" #end if #if $stability_scoring_options.nb_sections --nb_sections $stability_scoring_options.nb_sections #end if #if $stability_scoring_options.nb_candidates_per_section --nb_candidates_per_section $stability_scoring_options.nb_candidates_per_section #end if #if $stability_scoring_options.skip_genorm --skip_genorm $stability_scoring_options.skip_genorm #end if #if $stability_scoring_options.stability_score_weights --stability_score_weights "$stability_scoring_options.stability_score_weights" #end if #if $scalability_options.random_sampling_size --random_sampling_size $scalability_options.random_sampling_size #end if #if $scalability_options.random_sampling_seed --random_sampling_seed $scalability_options.random_sampling_seed #end if
&& zip -r results.zip results

section.name: input_output_options
section.title: Input/output options
section.help: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

param.name: species
param.label: Scientifc species name (genus and species)
param.help: At least genus and species name should be supplied. Words should be separated by ` ` or `_`. Note that character case is ignored. Examples: `--species 'Arabidopsis thaliana'`, `--species 'homo_sapiens' or `--species MARMOTA_MARMOTA_MARMOTA`.

param.name: samplesheet
param.label: Custom samplesheet (counts + designs)
param.help: Path to CSV / YAML file listing your own count samplesheet and their related experimental design. This file should be a comma-separated file with 4 columns (`counts`, `design`, `platform` and `normalised`). It must have a header row. Before running the pipeline, and for each count dataset provided by you, a design file with information about the samples in your experiment is required. Combine with --skip_fetch_eatlas_accessions if you only want to analyse your own count samplesheet. Otherwise, accessions from Expression Atlas and GEO will be fetched automatically. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/stableexpression/usage#samplesheet-input) for more information.

param.name: count_datasets
param.label: Count datasets
param.help: User count datasets in CSV format

param.name: experimental_designs
param.label: Experimental designs
param.help: Experimental designs relative to the provided count datasets

param.name: keywords
param.label: Keywords used for selecting specific Expression Atlas / GEO accessions
param.help: Keywords (separated by commas) to use when retrieving specific experiments from Expression Atlas and / or GEO datasets. The pipeline will select all Expression Atlas experiments / GEO datasets that contain the provided keywords in their description of in one of the condition names. Example: `--keywords 'stress,flowering'`. This parameter is unused if --skip_fetch_eatlas_accessions is set and --fetch_geo_accessions is not set.

param.name: target_genes
param.label: Target genes
param.help: One or multiple target genes (separated by commas). These can be gene IDs (as provided in your input datasets), Ensembl gene IDs, or gene symbols.

param.name: target_gene_file
param.label: File containing target genes
param.help: File containing one or multiple target genes (one ID per line). These can be gene IDs (as provided in your input datasets), Ensembl gene IDs, or gene symbols.

param.name: platform
param.label: Only download from this platform
param.help: By default, data from both RNA-seq and Microarray platforms are downloaded. Setting this parameter applies a filter to get data from only one of the two platforms. This filter is only used while fetching appropriate Expression atlas / GEO accessions. It will not filter accessions provided directly by the user.

param.name: accessions_only
param.label: Only get accessions from Expression Atlas / GEO and exit.
param.help: Use this option if you only want to get Expression Atlas accessions and skip the rest of the pipeline.

param.name: download_only
param.label: Only get accessions from Expression Atlas / GEO and download the selected datasets.
param.help: Use this option if you only want to get Expression Atlas / GEO accessions, download the selected data, and skip the rest of the pipeline.

section.name: public_data_options
section.title: Public data options
section.help: Options for fetching experiment data from Expression Atlas / GEO.

param.name: skip_fetch_eatlas_accessions
param.label: Skip fetching Expression Atlas accessions
param.help: Expression Atlas accessions are automatically fetched by default. Set this parameter to skip this step.

param.name: fetch_geo_accessions
param.label: Fetch GEO accessions from NCBI
param.help: Set this parameter to fetch GEO accessions from NCBI. This feature is experimental and may not work as expected. Please report any issues to https://github.com/nf-core/stableexpression.

param.name: accessions
param.label: Expression Atlas / GEO accession(s) to include
param.help: Provide Expression Atlas / GEO accession(s) that you want to download. The accessions should be comma-separated. Example: `--accessions E-MTAB-552,E-GEOD-61690,GSE8165,GSE8161`. Combine with --skip_fetch_eatlas_accessions if you want only these accessions to be used. User provided accessions are prioritised over excluded accessions.

param.name: accessions_file
param.label: File containing Expression Atlas / GEO accession(s) to download
param.help: File containing Expression Atlas / GEO accession(s) that you want to download. One accession per line. Example: `--accessions_file included_accessions.txt`. Combine with --skip_fetch_accessions if you want only these accessions to be used. User provided accessions are prioritised over excluded accessions.

param.name: excluded_accessions
param.label: Expression Atlas accession(s) to exclude
param.help: Provide Expression Atlas / GEO accession(s) that you want to exclude. The accessions should be comma-separated. Example: `--excluded_accessions E-MTAB-552,E-GEOD-61690`

param.name: excluded_accessions_file
param.label: File containing Expression Atlas accession(s) to exclude
param.help: File containing Expression Atlas / GEO accession(s) that you want to exclude. One accession per line. Example: `--excluded_accessions_file excluded_accessions.txt`.

param.name: gprofiler_target_db
param.label: Experimental: target database for g:Profiler
param.help: Experimental: target database for g:Profiler. You can see the full list of available target databases at https://biit.cs.ut.ee/gprofiler/convert.

param.name: gene_id_mapping
param.label: Custom gene id mapping file
param.help: Path to comma-separated file containing custom gene id mappings. Each row represents a mapping from the original gene ID in your count datasets to a prefered gene ID. The mapping file should be a comma-separated file with 2 columns (original_gene_id and gene_id) and a header row.

param.name: gene_metadata
param.label: Custom gene metadata file
param.help: Path to comma-separated file containing custom gene metadata information. Each row represents a gene and links its gene ID to its name and description. The metadata file should be a comma-separated file with 3 columns (gene_id, name and description) and a header row.

param.name: min_occurrence_quantile
param.label: Minimum quantile for the frequency of occurrence
param.help: To avoid genes that are rarely observed, genes less represented than the specified quantile will be filtered out. For example, value of 0.2 means that the 20% less represented will be filtered out. This filter is applied before using the absolute filter `--min_occurrence_freq`.

param.name: min_occurrence_freq
param.label: Minimum frequency of occurrence among all datasets
param.help: To avoid genes that are rarely observed, genes showing a frequency of occurrence below this threshold will be filtered out.

section.name: sample_filtering_options
section.title: Sample filtering options
section.help: Options for filtering samples based on their expression levels.

param.name: max_zero_ratio
param.label: Maximum ratio of zero counts to total counts
param.help: A filter is set up to avoid samples that contain a high proportion of zero counts. All samples with a ratio of zero counts to total counts above this threshold will be filtered out.

param.name: max_null_ratio
param.label: Maximum ratio of null values
param.help: A filter is set up to avoid samples that contain a high proportion of zero counts. All samples with a ratio of zero counts to total counts above this threshold will be filtered out.

param.name: max_null_ratio_valid_sample
param.label: Maximum ratio of null values in a sample for it to be considered in the computation of the null value malus
param.help: After filtering out samples with a very high proportion of zero counts (via `--max_null_ratio`), a second filter is set up to avoid samples that contain a substantial proportion of null values to be considered in the malus of null values comprised in the stability score.

section.name: statistical_options
section.title: Statistics options
section.help: Statistical options for normalisation and calculation of gene expression variation.

param.name: normalisation_method
param.label: Count normalisation method
param.help: Raw RNAseq data must be normalised before further processing. `tmp offers a more accurate representation of gene expression levels as it is unbiased toward gene length. However, you can choose `cpm` if you do not have access to a genome annotation.

param.name: gff
param.label: Genome annotation file (GFF format)
param.help: Path to genome annotation file (GFF format). Cannot be compressed.

param.name: gene_length
param.label: Gene length file
param.help: Path to comma-separated file containing gene lengths. Each row represents a gene and gives the length of its longest transcript. The file should be a comma-separated file with 2 columns (gene_id and length) and a header row.

param.name: quantile_norm_target_distrib
param.label: Target distribution for quantile normalisation
param.help: In order to compare counts between samples and different datasets, all normalised counts are quantile normalised and mapped to a specific distribution. The pipeline uses scikit-learn's quantile_transform function. You can select the target distribution to map counts to.

param.name: missing_value_imputer
param.label: Type of imputation method to use for missing values
param.help: The pipeline provides three options for imputing missing values: iterative, k-nearest neighbours, and gene mean. Iterative imputation uses a bayesian iterative algorithm to fill in missing values. K-nearest neighbors imputation uses a k-nearest neighbors algorithm to fill in missing values. Gene mean imputation is a very basic method that replaces missing values with the mean expression level of the gene across all samples.

section.name: stability_scoring_options
section.title: Stability scoring options
section.help: Options relative to assessment of stability for each gene.

param.name: nb_sections
param.label: Number of sections to divide genes into for stability scoring.
param.help: All genes are divided into sections based on their expression levels. Set this parameter to modify the number of sections.

param.name: nb_candidates_per_section
param.label: Number of candidate genes to keep for stability scoring in each section
param.help: Number of candidate genes to keep in each section for stability scoring. Within each section, the top candidates are selected based on the descriptor chosen with `--candidate_selection_descriptor`.

param.name: stability_score_weights
param.label: Weights for stability score calculation
param.help: Weights for Normfinder / Genorm / Coefficient of Variation (CV) / Robust Coefficient of Variation on Median (RCVM) respectively. Must be a comma-separated string. Example: 0.5,0.5,0.0,0

section.name: scalability_options
section.title: Scalability options
section.help: Options to improve pipeline scalability and robustness

param.name: random_sampling_size
param.label: Number of public dataset samples to choose randomly before downloading.
param.help: When dealing with species for which there is a large number (eg. >10000) of samples considering all the downloaded datasets, users may encounter RAM issues (eg. errors with `137` exit codes). In such cases, it is recommended to sample a random subset of these datasets to reduce the computational load. A first subsampling is performedduring the search for Expression Atlas accessions. In case there is still room for datasets and if the `--fetch_geo_accessions` flag was set, a second ssubsampling is performed during the search for NCBI GEO accessions.

param.name: random_sampling_seed
param.label: Seed for dataset random sampling.
param.help: Seed for dataset random sampling. This ensures reproducibility of the random sampling process. Changing the seed will result in a different random sample being selected.

citations.citation: @misc{nf-core/stableexpression, author = {Coen, Olivier}, year = {2025}, title = {nf-core/stableexpression}, publisher = {GitHub}, journal = {GitHub repository}, url = {https://github.com/OlivierCoen/stableexpression}, }
